twofa/otp

      • provisioningUri(self: TOTP): string
        timecode
      • timecode(interval: int; timestamp: int): int

      • verify(self: TOTP; otp: int; timestamp: int = 0; window: int = defaultWindow): bool
  • Exports

One Time Password (OTP) implementation following RFC 4226 (HOTP) and RFC 6238 (TOTP) specifications.

Supported HMAC algorithms:

  • algSHA1 — RFC 4226 standard, computed via openssl CLI
  • algSHA512 — Stronger alternative, computed via openssl CLI

Basic usage:

# TOTP with SHA1 (compatible with Google Authenticator)
let totp = initTotp("JBSWY3DPEHPK3PXP", issuer = "MyApp", accountName = "user@example.com")
echo totp.now()

# TOTP with SHA512
let totp512 = initTotp("JBSWY3DPEHPK3PXP", algorithm = algSHA512)
echo totp512.now()

# HOTP (counter-based)
let hotp = initHotp("JBSWY3DPEHPK3PXP", issuer = "MyApp", accountName = "user@example.com")
echo hotp.at(0)

Imports

base32

Types

HOTP = object of OneTimePassword
  counter*: int              ## Current counter value
HMAC-based One Time Password (RFC 4226). Counter-based: each call to at uses a counter value. 
OTPAlgorithm = enum
  algSHA1, algSHA512

Selects the HMAC hashing algorithm.

algSHA1 — RFC 4226 standard; required for compatibility with Google Authenticator and most TOTP apps

algSHA512 — Stronger alternative per RFC 6238 §1.2

OTPDefect = object of Defect
Raised for unrecoverable OTP programming errors 
OTPDigits = range[6 .. 8]
Valid OTP code lengths: 6, 7, or 8 digits per RFC 4226 
OTPError = object of CatchableError
Raised for recoverable OTP errors (e.g. invalid secret length, openssl not found, unexpected openssl output) 
TOTP = object of OneTimePassword
  interval*: int             ## Time step in seconds (default: 30)
Time-based One Time Password (RFC 6238). Generates codes based on the current Unix timestamp.

Consts

defaultDigits = 6
Standard OTP length per RFC 4226 
defaultInterval = 30
Default TOTP time step in seconds per RFC 6238 
defaultWindow = 1
Default verification window — number of time steps checked before and after the current one to tolerate clock drift 
secretSize {.intDefine: "otp.secretSize".} = 128
Maximum allowed secret length. Override at compile time with -d:otp.secretSize=256

Procs

proc at(self: HOTP; counter: int): int {.
    ...raises: [Base32Error, OSError, IOError, OTPError],
    tags: [ExecIOEffect, ReadIOEffect, RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Generates an HOTP code for the given counter value. 
proc at(self: TOTP; timestamp: int): int {.
    ...raises: [Base32Error, OSError, IOError, OTPError],
    tags: [ExecIOEffect, ReadIOEffect, RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Generates a TOTP code for the given Unix timestamp. 
proc generateCode(secret: string; input: int; digits: OTPDigits;
                  algorithm: OTPAlgorithm): int {.inline,
    ...raises: [Base32Error, OSError, IOError, OTPError],
    tags: [ExecIOEffect, ReadIOEffect, RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Decodes the Base32 secret, computes HMAC over the counter/timecode message using the selected algorithm, then truncates to digits. 
proc initHotp(secret: openArray[char]; digits: OTPDigits = defaultDigits;
              algorithm: OTPAlgorithm = algSHA1; issuer: string = "";
              accountName: string = ""; counter: int = 0): HOTP {.
    ...raises: [OTPError], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Creates an HOTP instance with a runtime secret. Raises OTPError if the secret exceeds secretSize.

secret — Base32-encoded shared secret digits — Code length: 6, 7, or 8 (default: 6) algorithm — algSHA1 (default, RFC 4226) or algSHA512 issuer — Service name shown in authenticator apps accountName — User account identifier counter — Initial counter value (default: 0)

proc initHotp(secret: static openArray[char]; digits: OTPDigits = defaultDigits;
              algorithm: OTPAlgorithm = algSHA1; issuer: string = "";
              accountName: string = ""; counter: int = 0): HOTP
Creates an HOTP instance with a compile-time secret. Emits a compile error if the secret exceeds secretSize. 
proc initTotp(secret: openArray[char]; digits: OTPDigits = defaultDigits;
              interval: int = defaultInterval;
              algorithm: OTPAlgorithm = algSHA1; issuer: string = "";
              accountName: string = ""): TOTP {....raises: [OTPError], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

Creates a TOTP instance with a runtime secret. Raises OTPError if the secret exceeds secretSize.

secret — Base32-encoded shared secret digits — Code length: 6, 7, or 8 (default: 6) interval — Time step in seconds (default: 30) algorithm — algSHA1 (default, RFC 4226) or algSHA512 issuer — Service name shown in authenticator apps accountName — User account identifier

proc initTotp(secret: static openArray[char]; digits: OTPDigits = defaultDigits;
              interval: int = defaultInterval;
              algorithm: OTPAlgorithm = algSHA1; issuer: string = "";
              accountName: string = ""): TOTP
Creates a TOTP instance with a compile-time secret. Emits a compile error if the secret exceeds secretSize. 
proc next(self: var HOTP): int {....raises: [Base32Error, OSError, IOError,
    OTPError], tags: [ExecIOEffect, ReadIOEffect, RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Generates the next HOTP code and advances the internal counter. Use this for sequential HOTP flows instead of at. 
proc now(self: TOTP): int {....raises: [Base32Error, OSError, IOError, OTPError], tags: [
    ExecIOEffect, ReadIOEffect, RootEffect, TimeEffect], forbids: [].}
Generates a TOTP code for the current system time. 
proc provisioningUri(self: HOTP; initialCount: int = 0): string {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Generates an otpauth://hotp/ URI suitable for QR code provisioning. Follows the Google Authenticator Key Uri Format: https://github.com/google/google-authenticator/wiki/Key-Uri-Format 
proc provisioningUri(self: TOTP): string {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Generates an otpauth://totp/ URI suitable for QR code provisioning. Follows the Google Authenticator Key Uri Format: https://github.com/google/google-authenticator/wiki/Key-Uri-Format 
proc timecode(interval: int; timestamp: int): int {.inline, ...raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Returns the TOTP time step index for the given Unix timestamp. 
proc verify(self: HOTP; otp: int; counter: int = 0): bool {.
    ...raises: [Base32Error, OSError, IOError, OTPError],
    tags: [ExecIOEffect, ReadIOEffect, RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Verifies an HOTP otp against the given counter value. 
proc verify(self: TOTP; otp: int; timestamp: int = 0;
            window: int = defaultWindow): bool {.
    ...raises: [Base32Error, OSError, IOError, OTPError],
    tags: [TimeEffect, ExecIOEffect, ReadIOEffect, RootEffect], forbids: [].}

Verifies a TOTP otp against a timestamp (defaults to now).

window — number of time steps checked on either side of the current step to tolerate clock drift between client and server. A window of 1 accepts codes from the previous, current, and next interval.

Exports

decode, encode, VERSION, Base32Error

Made with Nim. Generated: 2026-06-02 09:20:39 UTC
